title: Dependency Resolver — Rules & Watch Graph

classes.name: Rule
concrete_methods: getPrettyString(RepositorySet, Request, Pool, bool, array $installedMap, array $learnedPool): string
protected formatPackagesUnique(Pool, array, bool, ?ConstraintInterface, bool): string

classes.name: GenericRule
methods: getHash(): int — xxh3/sha1 hash of imploded literals
classes.notes: Used for require rules (n literals) and install-one-of rules

classes.name: Rule2Literals
methods: equals(Rule $rule): bool — fast-case for Rule2Literals instance
classes.notes: Optimized rule for exactly 2 literals (conflict rules). Always isAssertion()=false

classes.name: MultiConflictRule
methods: equals(Rule $rule): bool — only equal to other MultiConflictRule with same literals
classes.notes: MultiConflictRule([A,B,C]) semantically = Rule([-A,-B]) + Rule([-A,-C]) + Rule([-B,-C]). All literals watched in RuleWatchGraph.

classes.name: RuleSet
methods: add(Rule $rule, TYPE_* $type): void — dedup via hash+equals, assigns nextRuleId

classes.name: RuleSetIterator
methods: next(): void — advances offset, moves to next type when exhausted
rewind(): void — skips empty type buckets
classes.notes: Iterates in type priority order: PACKAGE(0) → REQUEST(1) → LEARNED(4)

classes.name: RuleSetGenerator
methods: protected addRulesForPackage(BasePackage, PlatformRequirementFilterInterface): void — BFS via SplQueue
protected createRequireRule(BasePackage, BasePackage[], RULE_*, $reasonData): ?Rule — returns new GenericRule
protected createInstallOneOfRule(BasePackage[], RULE_*, $reasonData): Rule — returns new GenericRule
protected createRule2Literals(BasePackage $issuer, BasePackage $provider, RULE_*, $reasonData): ?Rule — returns new Rule2Literals
protected createMultiConflictRule(BasePackage[], RULE_*, $reasonData): Rule — returns Rule2Literals if 2 packages, else MultiConflictRule
private addRule(TYPE_*, ?Rule): void — delegates to RuleSet::add()
rule_creation_logic: createMultiConflictRule → Rule2Literals (2 pkgs) OR MultiConflictRule (3+ pkgs)

classes.name: RuleWatchGraph
methods: insert(RuleWatchNode $node): void — skips assertions; MultiConflictRule watches all literals; others watch watch1+watch2
propagateLiteral(int $decidedLiteral, int $level, Decisions $decisions): ?Rule — returns conflicting Rule or null
classes.propagation_logic: Inverts decidedLiteral, walks watchChain; for non-MultiConflict: finds alternative literal or propagates unit clause; for MultiConflict: decides all other literals; returns conflict rule if found

classes.name: RuleWatchNode
methods: watch2OnHighest(Decisions $decisions): void — updates watch2 to highest-level literal (for learned rules, min 3 literals, non-MultiConflict)
getOtherWatch(int $literal): int — returns the watch that isn't $literal
moveWatch(int $from, int $to): void — updates watch1 or watch2

classes.name: RuleWatchChain
methods: seek(int $offset): void — rewinds then advances to offset
remove(): void — removes current element, seeks back to same offset
classes.notes: Extends SplDoublyLinkedList to add seek/remove-current. Used as per-literal linked list of watching RuleWatchNodes.

relationships: RuleSet stores Rule[] grouped by TYPE_* (composition)
RuleSetGenerator depends_on PolicyInterface, Pool, Request, PlatformRequirementFilterInterface
external_dependencies: Decisions — SAT solver decision tracking (decide, satisfy, conflict, decisionLevel)
Link — package dependency link (getTarget, getConstraint, getSource)
key_design_notes: Rule uses a compact bitfield for type (bits 0-7), reason (bits 8-15), and disabled flag (bits 16-23)
Rule2Literals is an optimization over GenericRule for the very common 2-literal conflict case
MultiConflictRule cannot be disabled (throws RuntimeException) and is treated specially in watch graph (all literals watched)
RuleSet deduplicates rules via hash bucket + equals() before insertion
RuleSetIterator yields rules in priority order: TYPE_PACKAGE(0) < TYPE_REQUEST(1) < TYPE_LEARNED(4)
RuleSetGenerator entry point is getRulesFor(); clears addedMap/addedPackagesByNames after generation
RuleWatchGraph implements 2-watched-literal propagation (DPLL/CDCL style unit propagation)
RuleWatchNode.watch2OnHighest() is called for learned rules to optimize backjumping
